threatLevel: Rockwell Automation / Schneider Electric / Siemens PLCs
executiveSummary: Active OT targeting by Iranian‑affiliated actors is exploiting internet‑exposed PLCs (Rockwell, Schneider, Siemens), causing operational disruption across Water, Energy, and Government Services. Remove direct internet access, validate controller logic integrity, and enforce access controls immediately. Security Advisory Rapid exploitation of on‑prem Microsoft SharePoint deserialization flaws enables machine‑key theft and RCE, with CISA urging hardening and patching; multiple SharePoint and ADFS items are now in KEV. Expect lateral movement toward OT via identity compromise. Security Advisory KEV Update Authentication bypasses on critical VPN gateways (Check Point Remote Access; PAN‑OS GlobalProtect CAS profiles) risk direct ingress to enterprise and potentially OT networks. Disable legacy/profiles and patch urgently. Alert Alert EV charging infrastructure (EVbee DC‑80) has multiple unauthenticated RCE/command injection paths (web, Bluetooth, OCPP), creating safety and grid‑impact risk; immediate isolation and hardening are required. View Article Nation‑state and proxy infrastructure: China‑nexus actors (incl. Volt Typhoon) are leveraging covert SOHO/IoT proxy networks for CNI pre‑positioning; allied advisory urges urgent edge hardening and egress controls. Supporting Report CERT Polska disclosed critical flaws in ICU Scandinavia Boomerang monitoring: plaintext credentials via HTTP and unauthorized sensor DB writes—patch and rotate creds immediately. View Article Multiple ICS‑relevant CVEs (Delta PLC Modbus, PcVue credentials, DAQFactory RCE, EIP/CIP stack DoS) raise near‑term risk to manufacturing and water sector operations. CVE Summary CVE Summary Fortinet perimeter exposure: large‑scale FortiGate credential leakage (“FortiBleed”) with continued validity in some cases—rotate secrets, review logs, and enforce PBKDF2 hashing. Alert

descriptions: Initial Access: VPN auth bypass (T1133 External Remote Services), Exploit Public‑Facing App (T1190), Exposed VNC/HMI (T1021.001)
Execution/Persistence: Webshells on SharePoint/GeoServer (T1505.003), Scheduled Tasks (T1053), DLL search‑order hijacking (Windows) (T1574.001)
Defense Evasion: OAuth/Service Principal abuse in M365 Graph (T1550.001/T1098.001), AMSI/WAF evasion via deserialization
Command & Control: DNS tunneling/AAAA channeling (T1071.004), Covert SOHO/IoT proxy chains
ICS‑Specific: Unauthorized Modbus/CIP commands, PLC mode changes, AOI tampering; OCPP DataTransfer injection

outlook72h: Expect continued automated probing of SharePoint endpoints following PoC circulation; prioritize patch/validation and WAF rulesets on exposed portals. Monitor GlobalProtect and Check Point RA auth logs for anomalous sessions; temporarily restrict access to allow‑listed IPs where feasible. Increase OT perimeter monitoring for Modbus/CIP anomalies and VNC scans; enforce account lockouts and credential rotation on internet‑facing services. EV charging/OCPP operators should deploy emergency ACLs and mTLS enforcement; review firmware signing policies.

Remove direct internet access, validate controller logic integrity, and enforce access controls immediately. Security Advisory Rapid exploitation of on‑prem Microsoft SharePoint deserialization flaws enables machine‑key theft and RCE, with CISA urging hardening and patching; multiple SharePoint and ADFS items are now in KEV. Expect lateral movement toward OT via identity compromise. Security Advisory KEV Update Authentication bypasses on critical VPN gateways (Check Point Remote Access; PAN‑OS GlobalProtect CAS profiles) risk direct ingress to enterprise and potentially OT networks. Disable legacy/profiles and patch urgently. Alert Alert EV charging infrastructure (EVbee DC‑80) has multiple unauthenticated RCE/command injection paths (web, Bluetooth, OCPP), creating safety and grid‑impact risk; immediate isolation and hardening are required. View Article Nation‑state and proxy infrastructure: China‑nexus actors (incl. Volt Typhoon) are leveraging covert SOHO/IoT proxy networks for CNI pre‑positioning; allied advisory urges urgent edge hardening and egress controls. Supporting Report CERT Polska disclosed critical flaws in ICU Scandinavia Boomerang monitoring: plaintext credentials via HTTP and unauthorized sensor DB writes—patch and rotate creds immediately. View Article Multiple ICS‑relevant CVEs (Delta PLC Modbus, PcVue credentials, DAQFactory RCE, EIP/CIP stack DoS) raise near‑term risk to manufacturing and water sector operations. CVE Summary CVE Summary Fortinet perimeter exposure: large‑scale FortiGate credential leakage (“FortiBleed”) with continued validity in some cases—rotate secrets, review logs, and enforce PBKDF2 hashing. Alert Key Findings OT‑native threat: Direct PLC tampering and HMI data manipulation confirmed across U.S. critical sectors; actors abused native engineering tools and exposed services. Security Advisory Perimeter auth bypasses on enterprise VPN/portals (Check Point, PAN‑OS) create low‑friction paths to OT via IT gateways and jump hosts. View Article View Article EV infrastructure is exploitable at multiple layers (OCPP, firmware, Bluetooth), enabling unsafe charging control and charger outages. CVE Bulletin SharePoint on‑prem RCE is being exploited in the wild to steal IIS machine keys and persist via deserialization paths—hardening and credential rotation are essential. Security Advisory Facility monitoring software (ICU Boomerang) exposes plaintext credentials and unauth write access to sensor databases, risking plant data integrity. View Article Threat Dashboard 5 Actively exploited KEV items affecting edge/identity KEV focus 9 OT/ICS vendor advisories of note Patch planning 3 Nation‑state activity clusters (recent) Watchlisted Top Five Priority Threats 1) Iranian‑affiliated actors exploiting internet‑exposed PLCs (Rockwell, Schneider, Siemens) — Immediate removal from internet, logic integrity validation, and strict access controls are required to prevent further tampering and service disruption. Security Advisory 2) Check Point RA/Mobile Access CVE‑2026‑50751 (active exploitation) — Disable IKEv1 and legacy clients, require machine certificates, patch to fixed releases, and hunt for suspicious RA logins. Alert 3) PAN‑OS GlobalProtect CAS auth bypass CVE‑2026‑0265 — Remove CAS auth profiles from login interfaces until patched; restrict public exposure and enforce device certificates/MFA. Alert 4) SharePoint on‑prem RCE/deserialization (multiple CVEs; active exploitation) — Patch immediately, enable AMSI Full Mode, rotate machine keys/service creds, hunt for webshells. Security Advisory 5) EV charging infrastructure (EVbee DC‑80) multi‑vector compromise — Isolate chargers, enforce mutual TLS/OCPP controls, disable unsigned firmware updates, and monitor for OCPP DataTransfer abuse. CVE Bulletin OT/ICS Relevance Assessment Direct ICS impact confirmed (AA26‑097a): controller logic edits, AOI tampering, and HMI manipulation in multiple sectors. Perimeter to OT pivot risk remains elevated: active exploitation of SharePoint/ADFS and VPN auth bypasses can bridge to industrial DMZs and jump hosts. ICS protocol exposure persists (Modbus/CIP/S7): new PLC/stack CVEs and misconfigurations raise DoS and unauthorized command risk. Supply chain and remote‑service dependencies (EV charging, RUGGEDCOM APE + Virtual NGFW) can undermine OT perimeters if not segmented and hardened. Threat Actor Activity Iran‑affiliated groups targeting U.S. OT via exposed PLCs; use of leased infra and native vendor tools. Security Advisory China‑nexus (Volt Typhoon et al.) leveraging SOHO/IoT proxy fleets for CNI pre‑positioning. Advisory OilRig/APT34 “Project CAV3RN” abusing Microsoft Graph calendar events and DNS AAAA for C2—stealthy persistence in M365 estates. Research Pro‑Russia hacktivists exploiting exposed VNC to deface/alter HMI setpoints (loss of view). Advisory State exploitation of network edge/routers (PRC; FSB Center 16) to exfiltrate configs, harvest credentials, and mirror traffic. CSA CSA Vulnerability and CVE Watch Delta DVP12SE PLC Modbus unauth access/DoS; DTMSoft unsafe deserialization (RCE). CVE Summary PcVue ( CVE Summary EVbee DC‑80 multiple CVEs (web/Bluetooth/OCPP/firmware) enabling RCE and operational control. CVE Summary PAN‑OS CVE‑2026‑0265 (CAS) and Check Point CVE‑2026‑50751 (RA) — active/in‑scope auth bypasses. PAN‑OS Alert Check Point Alert SharePoint/ADFS multiple CVEs under active exploitation; machine‑key theft and RCE observed. CISA Tycon TPDIN‑Monitor‑WEB2 auth bypass/cleartext secrets; Siemens SIDIS SmartPlug critical vulns. ICS Advisory ICS Advisory ICU Scandinavia Boomerang (≤2.4.18.028) plaintext credentials and unauthorized writes. CERT Advisory Affected Vendors and Technologies Rockwell Automation / Schneider Electric / Siemens PLCs Palo Alto Networks PAN‑OS (GlobalProtect CAS) Check Point Security Gateways (RA/Mobile Access) Microsoft SharePoint / ADFS / IIS ICU Scandinavia Boomerang EVbee DC‑80 (OCPP/firmware/Bluetooth) Delta Electronics PLC & Tools PcVue HMI/SCADA Siemens RUGGEDCOM APE + Virtual NGFW Siemens SIDIS Secured SmartPlug Tycon TPDIN‑Monitor‑WEB2 GeoServer (CVE‑2024‑36401) Fortinet FortiGate / FortiSandbox Affected Sectors Water and Wastewater Systems; Energy (generation, T&D); Government Services and Facilities Transportation (EV charging infrastructure, telecom backbones); Critical Manufacturing Building Automation (KNX), Biometric/Facility systems Public sector GIS (GeoServer) supporting utilities and emergency services TTPs and MITRE ATT&CK-style observations Initial Access: VPN auth bypass (T1133 External Remote Services), Exploit Public‑Facing App (T1190), Exposed VNC/HMI (T1021.001) Execution/Persistence: Webshells on SharePoint/GeoServer (T1505.003), Scheduled Tasks (T1053), DLL search‑order hijacking (Windows) (T1574.001) Defense Evasion: OAuth/Service Principal abuse in M365 Graph (T1550.001/T1098.001), AMSI/WAF evasion via deserialization Command & Control: DNS tunneling/AAAA channeling (T1071.004), Covert SOHO/IoT proxy chains ICS‑Specific: Unauthorized Modbus/CIP commands, PLC mode changes, AOI tampering; OCPP DataTransfer injection Defensive Mitigation Priorities Remove OT devices from direct internet exposure; enforce industrial DMZ segmentation and monitored jump hosts. Security Advisory Patch and harden SharePoint/ADFS; enable AMSI Full Mode; rotate machine keys and service credentials; hunt for webshells. Security Advisory Disable legacy RA settings and patch VPN gateways (Check Point IKEv1; PAN‑OS CAS). Gate remote access with phishing‑resistant MFA. JPCERT JPCERT Isolate and harden EV charging/OCPP networks; enforce mTLS, disable unsigned firmware, and monitor for DataTransfer abuse. CISA Bulletin Rotate Fortinet/FortiGate credentials; upgrade to PBKDF2 hashing; audit for config exports and anomalous admin access. JPCERT Apply ICS advisories for Tycon TPDIN‑Monitor‑WEB2 and Siemens SIDIS SmartPlug; remove default accounts and minimize exposure. ICS Advisory ICS Advisory Patch ICU Boomerang to ≥2.4.18.029; rotate all exposed service/SMTP creds; restrict static file exposure. CERT Advisory Next 72‑Hour Outlook Expect continued automated probing of SharePoint endpoints following PoC circulation; prioritize patch/validation and WAF rulesets on exposed portals. Monitor GlobalProtect and Check Point RA auth logs for anomalous sessions; temporarily restrict access to allow‑listed IPs where feasible. Increase OT perimeter monitoring for Modbus/CIP anomalies and VNC scans; enforce account lockouts and credential rotation on internet‑facing services. EV charging/OCPP operators should deploy emergency ACLs and mTLS enforcement; review firmware signing policies.
